toolTip:
Crystal list is displayed here.
To add a new crystal, set the new crystal data in the 'Crystal Information' panel and click the 'Add' button.
To modify a crystal data, select the target crystal, modify it in the 'Crystal Information panel', and click the 'Replace' button.
To remove a crystal from the list, select the target crystal and click the 'Delete' button.
To delete all crystals in the list, click the 'All Clear' button.

text: Ug: Crystal potential for elastic scattering

toolTip: Angular resolution of incident electrons in mrad.

Sets the thickness to slice the sample when calculating the TDS electron intensity.
Smaller values result in slower calculation speed but higher accuracy.

Linear image model. Low comutation costs.
This model is preferable to be applied for a thin sample 
in which the weak phase object approximation holds

Transmission cross coefficient (TCC) model. High computaion costs.
This model should be used for a strong scattering object which usually
 excites many strong diffracted beams.
